title: Reading Aircraft Labels
disclaimer: Training support only. Confirm local label conventions and operating procedures with an instructor.
learning_objectives: Identify callsign and flight level from an aircraft label.
Use traffic flow color as a quick scan aid.
Read heading vectors without losing the planned route picture.
key_points: Callsign and FL are the first items to confirm before issuing an instruction.
Color helps classify traffic flow, but it should not replace route and altitude checks.
The heading vector shows where the aircraft is currently pointing.

lesson_steps.title: Label Priority
lesson_steps.body: Start with callsign and FL. Then confirm route, flow, and heading before deciding whether action is needed.

lesson_steps.title: Map Scan
lesson_steps.body: Compare aircraft labels, colors, route lines, and heading vectors. Avoid focusing on one label for too long.

lesson_steps.title: Label Reading Drill
script: Open the Training Alpha beginner mix.
Select three aircraft in different traffic flows.
Read callsign, FL, route, and heading before issuing any instruction.

exercise.title: Read And Confirm Three Labels
exercise.objective: Correctly identify callsign, FL, route, traffic flow, and heading for three aircraft.
student_actions: Select an arrival, departure, and overflight.
Read each label and selected-aircraft metadata.
Confirm whether each aircraft is established on its planned route.
success_conditions: Three aircraft are identified by callsign and FL.
The student correctly separates label information from route information.
debrief_prompts: Which label item did you check first?
Did color help or distract from the actual route picture?
Was any aircraft heading different from its route?
